items.text:
@craftycasey

Guys, I finally finished the floating bookshelf for my dorm room and I'm obsessed!! I've been saving scrap pine from the Fenway woodshop for weeks, and last Saturday I sanded, stained, and mounted the whole thing in one afternoon. The only thing I actually paid for was a $6 pack of L-brackets from the hardware store on Third Street — everything else was free wood the shop was tossing out!

The trickiest part was leveling it. My first two holes were way off and I had to patch them, but the third time it sat perfectly flush against the wall. Honestly, if you've ever thought woodworking looks intimidating, it's really not — you just have to be patient and measure twice before you drill.

Anyone else been building stuff for their room this semester? Drop a photo below, I want to steal your ideas!! #dormDIY #woodshop

questions.stem: According to the post, what did Casey pay for to complete the project?

options.A: A can of wood stain from the campus woodshop
options.B: A sheet of pine from the store on Third Street
options.C: A leveling tool borrowed from a dorm neighbor
options.D: A pack of brackets from a hardware store

questions.explanation: Casey writes that the only thing they actually paid for was a $6 pack of L-brackets from the hardware store on Third Street; the wood, stain, and everything else were free.

questions.stem: The word "flush" in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Casey describes the shelf finally sitting "perfectly flush against the wall" after two failed tries, meaning it sat evenly and level against the surface.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Casey's attitude toward woodworking?

options.A: They regret starting such a difficult project.
options.B: They feel confident enough to encourage others to try.
options.C: They plan to hire someone for the next build.
options.D: They intend to sell their shelves to classmates.

questions.explanation: Casey tells followers that woodworking "is really not" intimidating and invites others to share their own projects, showing enough confidence to encourage beginners rather than regret (A) or outsource the work (C).

items.text:
Subject: Parking Permit Renewal for the 2026–2027 Academic Year

Dear Commuter Student,

Your current parking permit expires on July 31, so it's time to renew for the coming year. All returning commuters must complete their renewal through the Transportation Services portal before August 15 to keep the same lot assignment. Renewals submitted after that date will be processed in the order received, and the popular lots near Kessler Hall tend to fill up quickly.

The annual permit fee is $120, which you can pay online by card or charge to your student account. Once your payment clears, your digital permit activates automatically — there's no longer a physical sticker to pick up at our office. If you're studying abroad in the fall and won't need a spot until January, just reply to this message and we'll place a hold on your account instead of charging you now.

Questions? Stop by the Transportation Services desk in Room 110 of the Hobbes Center, open weekdays from 8:30 AM to 4:00 PM.

Best regards,
Dana Ruiz
Transportation Services

format_metadata.subject: Parking Permit Renewal for the 2026–2027 Academic Year

questions.stem: According to the email, what must returning commuters do to keep their current lot assignment?

options.A: To pay the $120 fee at the Transportation Services desk
options.B: To pick up a new physical sticker before July 31
options.C: To complete the renewal through the portal before August 15
options.D: To reply to the message asking for an account hold

questions.explanation: The email states that all returning commuters must complete their renewal through the Transportation Services portal before August 15 to keep the same lot assignment.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who submit their renewal after August 15?

options.A: They will receive a partial refund of the permit fee.
options.B: They may not be given their preferred parking lot.
options.C: They must collect a sticker from the Hobbes Center.
options.D: They will be assigned a spot far from Kessler Hall.

questions.explanation: Because late renewals are processed in the order received and the popular lots near Kessler Hall fill up quickly, students renewing after August 15 may not get their preferred lot. The email never mentions refunds (A) or a guaranteed distant assignment (D).

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind commuter students to renew their parking permits
options.B: To announce a new physical parking sticker program
options.C: To promote the parking lots located near Kessler Hall
options.D: To explain the process for studying abroad in the fall

questions.explanation: The email's overall purpose is to remind commuter students that their permits expire and to guide them through renewing for the new year. Option B reverses the text (there is no longer a sticker), and C and D each capture only a minor detail.
